channel.title: Race on purpleidea.com
channel.description: Recent content in Race on purpleidea.com

item.title: Debugging golang programs
item.description: I’ve been writing a lot of golang lately. I’ve hit painful problems in the past. Here are some debugging tips. Hopefully they help you out. I bet you don’t know #2. #0 Use log.Printf: This should go without saying, but I’m ashamed to say it’s what I use the most. We’ve only been C programming for 44+ years, and it’s still what is most useful! #1 Use go run -race:

item.title: Golang parallelism issues causing “too many open files” error
item.description: I’ve been hacking in golang for a while, but I’ll admit that I didn’t get too deep into some of the language nuances until more recently. Since some of them have started to bite me, here’s a little post-mortem of one of the problems I was having. After hacking and testing code all day, I made a seemingly innocuous change, and when running my program, I saw the following error:
